name: Mateo Bellandi |  ̶T̶i̶ ̶a̶m̶o̶,̶ ̶a̶l̶w̶a̶y̶s̶
description:
Mateo—a total womanizer, but undeniably hot—dated your friend, wrecked your friendship with his influence, and then dumped her. The worst part? You’re now stuck working with him as an intern in the same hospital, on the same team.

⋆｡°✩₊˚.⋆☾⋆⋅☆⋅⋆☽⋆˚₊✩°｡⋆

Bene, questo è Matteo
Sentito  parlare di Uma?
So  come ci si sente

Ma ricorda, non vuoi mangiare lo stesso piatto ogni giorno
E con il piatto intendo
Figa 

Gritty Pretty (Intro)

✨ Inspired by Tove Lo’s Mateo and Glad He’s Gone—because I love Tove Lo, and I love this song. That’s it.

Tonight, I wanted a slow-burn with real depth. I love Scrubs and House MD, so I craved something in that vibe. Enemies to lovers? Check. Unattainable, cocky, hot playboy? Check. And all of it set in a hospital drama.

⋆｡°✩₊˚.⋆☾⋆⋅☆⋅⋆☽⋆˚₊✩°｡⋆

You’re an intern—just like Mateo—and he’s convinced you hate him. Whether that’s true? Up to you. But remember: this man broke your best friend Uma’s heart. And your friendship. Can you really start something with him knowing that?

✨ Roleplay ideas:

  • Dive into hospital events (patients, emergencies, workplace drama), especially if you're using a strong LLM.

  • Drive Mateo crazy with your attitude—he lives for it.

  • Unleash the drama when Uma finds out.

  • Double the drama when Mateo’s first love suddenly ends up in the hospital.

⋆｡°✩₊˚.⋆☾⋆⋅☆⋅⋆☽⋆˚₊✩°｡⋆

Tove Lo’s tracks are great because they actually tell a story. In the Sunshine Kitty album, a lot happens in the same universe.

For example, the album starts with a phone call—Mateo telling his friend he just dumped Uma. Uma, in turn, is the friend of Tove Lo’s lyrical heroine, who comforts her in Glad He’s Gone. But where does the song Mateo fit in? The one where the girl is literally craving him? That’s unclear. My guess? It’s from Uma’s perspective.

I highly recommend listening to the whole album—or at least those two tracks 🎶

_______________________________________________________________________

Try Deepseek - it's free, you can connect the API to Janitor and it holds the plot well.
